title: Summer Burgers
subtitle: with Pickled Jalapeños & Barbecue Fries
ingredients: ¼ cup Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Peel and slice the red onion into ½ inch thick rounds. Cut the russet potatoes into ½ inch thick wedges. Peel and mince the garlic.

Place the onion rounds on a baking sheet and toss with 2 tsp vegetable oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Push onion rounds to one side, and add the wedged potatoes, 2 tsp vegetable oil, BBQ seasoning, and a pinch of pepper to the other side of the baking sheet. Toss to coat. Roast until the barbecue fries are crispy and browned in places, 17 to 20 minutes.

Heat 2 tsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Once hot, add the burgers and cook until well browned, 5 to 7 minutes per side. Remove burgers from heat, top with Chao slices, and cover until cheese melts, 3 to 5 minutes. Slice the potato buns and toast directly on the oven rack, 3 to 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a small bowl. Mix the garlic aioli until combined.
Spread some garlic aioli on the toasted potato buns. Top with the burgers, roasted onions, and jalapeños. Serve with barbecue fries and any remaining garlic aioli for dipping. Dig in!

tips: TIP: Try to keep onion rounds intact to allow for even roasting.
TIP: If you prefer, you can also grill the burgers on medium heat for 5 to 7 minutes per side.
